ddls:
CREATE TABLE "job_postings" (
  "posting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job posting */,
  "title" INTEGER /* ID of the job title */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the company making the job posting */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location of the job posting */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job posting source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the job posting */,
  "experience_required" TEXT /* Experience level required (e.g., Entry, Mid, Senior) */,
  "education_required" TEXT /* Education level required (e.g., High School, Bachelor's, Master's) */,
  "remote_option" INTEGER /* Indicates if the job has a remote option */,
  "posted_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the job */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the job posting (e.g., Active, Inactive, Filled) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the job posting was created */,
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* ID of the salary range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("posting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_posted_by FOREIGN KEY ("posted_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_salary_id FOREIGN KEY ("salary_id") REFERENCES salary_ranges ("salary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_title FOREIGN KEY ("title") REFERENCES job_titles ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry the company operates in */,
  "website" TEXT /* Company website */,
  "size" TEXT /* Size of the company (e.g., Small, Medium, Large) */,
  "founded_year" INTEGER /* Year the company was founded */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Annual revenue of the company */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the company */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the job posting is located */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the job posting is located */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the job posting is located */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_titles" (
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "title" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category the job title belongs to (e.g., Engineering, Education) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job title */,
  "responsibilities" TEXT /* Key responsibilities associated with the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_ranges" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary range */,
  "posting_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job posting the salary range belongs to */,
  "min_salary" REAL /* Minimum salary for the job posting */,
  "max_salary" REAL /* Maximum salary for the job posting */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency of the salary range */,
  "benefits" TEXT /* Additional benefits offered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salary_ranges_posting_id FOREIGN KEY ("posting_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("posting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., job seeker, recruiter, admin) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "preferences" TEXT /* User preferences (e.g., preferred job categories, locations) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job application */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the job application */,
  "posting_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job posting being applied for */,
  "application_date" TEXT /* Date the job application was made */,
  "cover_letter" TEXT /* Cover letter submitted with the application */,
  "resume_url" TEXT /* URL to the user's resume */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the application (e.g., Submitted, Rejected, Interviewing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_applications_posting_id FOREIGN KEY ("posting_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("posting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_applications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job posting source */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the job posting source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the job posting source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job posting source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)
